title: Create the pure goal-presentation registry module with fitness and project entries and hoisted formatters
value: so that every recap/Today/progress/legend surface resolves kind-aware labels and stat slots from one client-safe seam instead of hardcoded fitness strings — the seam that unblocks the rest of the multi-domain work
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/lib/goal-presentation.ts is pure / client-safe: no Prisma import, no @/lib/calendar import, no Node built-ins (same purity class as src/lib/rarity-core.ts) — confirmed by importing it from a Satori JSX context with no server-only resolution error

Exports the TypeScript shapes exactly per plan-blueprint.md §1.3: StatFormat union (int|volumeLb|elevationFt|currency|ratioOfTotal|percent), StatSource declarative union (recapField|logLatest|scheduledItem|targetCurrent — NO closures, NO DB), StatSlot, HeaderStyle (program-week|weeks-to-target|none), GoalPresentation { kind, ringLabel, headerStyle, statSlots, restCopy, legendDefault }

Exports FITNESS_PRESENTATION per §1.4: ringLabel "READINESS", headerStyle "program-week", exactly 4 recapField statSlots keyed workouts/volume/prs/elevation with labels WORKOUTS/VOLUME/NEW PRs/ELEVATION and formats int/volumeLb/int/elevationFt, restCopy set to the generic recovery copy, legendDefault "fitness"

Exports PROJECT_PRESENTATION per §1.4: ringLabel "PROGRESS" (NOT "TRACTION" — that re-narrows the engine to startups), headerStyle "weeks-to-target", restCopy null, legendDefault "project", and exactly 2 statSlots — { key:"mrr", label:"MRR", source:{from:"logLatest", metricKey:"mrr"}, format:"currency" } and { key:"milestones", label:"MILESTONES", source:{from:"scheduledItem", itemType:"milestone", agg:"doneOverTotal"}, format:"ratioOfTotal" } — NO Subs/Conversion cells

presentationForGoal(goal) returns REGISTRY[goal.kind] when present, else a DEFAULT_PRESENTATION that is a clone of FITNESS_PRESENTATION with kind relabeled "__default__" (never "fitness") — matches the existing goal.kind ?? "fitness" defaulting in recap.ts and legend.ts while keeping neither vertical default-by-omission

fmtVolume and fmtElevation are defined and exported here (hoisted from recap-card.tsx:17-23) and produce byte-identical output: "2,370 lb" / "5,200 ft" for numbers and "—" for null — verified against the current recap-card.tsx implementations

Chewgether acceptance criterion: presentationForGoal({ kind: "project" }) returns ringLabel "PROGRESS" and statSlots of length 2 keyed exactly [mrr, milestones]; presentationForGoal({ kind: null }) returns kind "__default__" with the fitness 4-slot grid
npx tsc --noEmit passes, npm run lint passes, npm run build passes

title: Add statSlots, resolveStatSlot, gated project fetch, and weeks-to-target header fields to computeWeeklyRecap
value: so that the recap bundle carries kind-aware resolved stat slots plus a weeks-to-target header for project goals, while the fitness numbers and legacy fields stay byte-identical and no project query runs for a fitness goal
acceptanceCriteria: computeWeeklyRecap is restructured to goal-first fetch → presentationForGoal(goal) → a single gated Promise.all that runs the project queries ONLY when presentation.statSlots declare them (the existing Promise.all at recap.ts:180-211 cannot gate inside the block that fetches the goal itself — fetch order fix per blueprint §2.2)

Adds resolveStatSlot(slot, ctx) that switches on slot.source.from over already-fetched data (ctx: { recap legacy fields, logLatest Map, scheduledAgg Map, breakdown, targets }) and returns { key, label, value, isNull } — handling all four sources recapField / logLatest / scheduledItem / targetCurrent; no Prisma access inside the pure registry

WeeklyRecap gains statSlots: ResolvedStatSlot[]; the four legacy fields workoutsCompleted, volumeLb, prCount, hikeElevationFt REMAIN on the bundle (the MCP generate_recap_card stats JSON and the highlight logic at recap.ts:327-424 still read them)

logLatest source uses prisma.logEntry.findFirst({ where:{ goalId, metric:<key>, value:{not:null} }, orderBy:{ date:"desc" } }); scheduledAgg source uses prisma.scheduledItem.groupBy({ by:["status"], where:{ goalId, type:<itemType> } }) reduced to { done, total } — the Milestones stat sources from this ScheduledItem aggregate, NOT log:milestones_done (avoids the §0.2 desync; matches MilestoneBurnDown)
RecapProgramHeader gains weeksToTarget (number) and targetDateLabel (string) computed via @/lib/calendar (USER_TZ-correct), never raw Date math; populated only when headerStyle is weeks-to-target

Fitness byte-identical: for the Mt. Elbert goal, statSlots resolve to the same strings the card prints today (e.g. "2", "2,370 lb", "1", "5,200 ft") with isNull flags matching volumeLb===null / hikeElevationFt===null; the four legacy fields are unchanged
No project query (logEntry.findFirst / scheduledItem.groupBy) executes for a fitness focus goal — the gated Promise.all skips them

Chewgether acceptance criterion: computeWeeklyRecap for goal cmqbfseel0000cgdn3oz1uz2u yields statSlots [{ key:"mrr", value:"—", isNull:true }, { key:"milestones", value:"0/7", isNull:false }] and header weeksToTarget ≈ 15 with targetDateLabel reflecting 2026-09-30
npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, npm run build pass; MCP curl weekly_summary_data for the fitness goal returns the unchanged stats shape (4 legacy fields intact)
dependsOn: Create the pure goal-presentation registry module with fitness and project entries and hoisted formatters

title: Drive recap-card ring labels, headers, and stat grids from the presentation registry
value: so that the OG recap card renders the kind-correct ring label, header style, and stat grid for any goal from one presentation source — and the fitness card stays pixel-identical
acceptanceCriteria: recap-card.tsx resolves const presentation = presentationForGoal(recap.goal) once per slide (near line 300 and the SlideOne path) and imports fmtVolume/fmtElevation from goal-presentation.ts — the local copies at recap-card.tsx:17-23 are removed (one shared formatter, byte-identical fitness)
Both ring labels (lines 426 and 855) read presentation.ringLabel — "READINESS" for fitness, "PROGRESS" for project

Both program lines (lines 304-307 and 793-795) switch on presentation.headerStyle: program-week renders the current "WEEK n · DAY m OF d" string; weeks-to-target renders e.g. "15 WEEKS TO SEP 30" from recap.header.weeksToTarget / targetDateLabel; none omits the line

Both stat grids (lines 588-651 and 950-961) map recap.statSlots into StatCell (StatCell itself unchanged), rendering a single centered row when length 2 and a 2×2 when length 4 — no dash-padded fake 4-cell grid

Satori re-render verified: card renders with no Satori error, flex-only layout, inline styles, no conic-gradient, no CSS vars, SVG stroke-dasharray progress ring intact (per memory satori-no-conic-use-svg-arc)

Fitness byte-identical: the Mt. Elbert recap card at ≤390px is visually identical to pre-change — same ring label READINESS, same 2×2 WORKOUTS/VOLUME/NEW PRs/ELEVATION values and isNull dashes, same WEEK n · DAY m OF d header string
Chewgether acceptance criterion: the card for goal cmqbfseel0000cgdn3oz1uz2u renders a PROGRESS ring, a "weeks to SEP 30" header, and a single 2-cell row showing MRR "—" and MILESTONES "0/7"
npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, npm run build pass
dependsOn: Create the pure goal-presentation registry module with fitness and project entries and hoisted formatters
Add statSlots, resolveStatSlot, gated project fetch, and weeks-to-target header fields to computeWeeklyRecap

acceptanceCriteria: A Vitest spec (e.g. src/lib/goal-presentation.test.ts) drives resolveStatSlot per kind with synthetic ctx (no DB), asserting the fitness 4 slots resolve to the exact strings + isNull flags the card prints today (e.g. "2", "2,370 lb", "1", "5,200 ft"; null volume/elevation → "—" isNull:true)

Test asserts presentationForGoal for fitness returns ringLabel "READINESS", headerStyle "program-week", and slot order WORKOUTS/VOLUME/NEW PRs/ELEVATION; and that null/unknown kind returns kind "__default__" with the same 4 slots

Chewgether acceptance criterion: a project fixture (kind "project", targets log:mrr + log:milestones_done, logLatest mrr=null, scheduledAgg milestone {done:0,total:7}) resolves statSlots to [{ key:"mrr", value:"—", isNull:true }, { key:"milestones", value:"0/7", isNull:false }] with ringLabel "PROGRESS" and headerStyle "weeks-to-target"
Test confirms a milestone-progress fixture ({done:3,total:7}) resolves the milestones slot to "3/7" isNull:false, proving the ScheduledItem aggregate (not log:milestones_done) backs the stat
npx vitest run passes and the committed suite includes these cases alongside the existing food-units.test.ts setup
npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, npm run build pass

Drive recap-card ring labels, headers, and stat grids from the presentation registry

acceptanceCriteria: npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, and npm run build are all green on the integrated Sprint 6 changes

MCP curl tools/list succeeds; weekly_summary_data / generate_recap_card for the fitness Mt. Elbert goal returns the unchanged stats JSON (4 legacy fields workoutsCompleted/volumeLb/prCount/hikeElevationFt intact)

title: Drive Today rest-day copy from presentation.restCopy instead of hardcoded Mt. Elbert text
value: so that the rest-day recovery tip reflects each goal's kind (and project goals, which have no rest-day concept, show no tip) instead of always naming Mt. Elbert
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/page.tsx, resolve `const presentation = presentationForGoal(focusGoal)` near the existing focus-goal resolution (~line 23) and render the rest-day block body from `presentation.restCopy`.
The hardcoded 'Mt. Elbert' / aerobic-base literal string (page.tsx lines 247–254) is removed; copy text now comes from the registry (FITNESS_PRESENTATION.restCopy).

When `presentation.restCopy` is null (project kind / __default__ with null), the entire rest-day tip block is omitted — no empty `

`, no stray border.

Fitness un-regressed: for the fitness focus goal the rendered rest-day tip is pixel-identical to current production (same copy text via FITNESS_PRESENTATION.restCopy, same `isRestDay` gating, same Tailwind classes/border).
Project surfaces no longer mislabel: a project (Chewgether-kind) focus goal never renders the Mt. Elbert recovery tip on Today.
page.tsx stays a server component (no new `"use client"`); any date math continues through `@/lib/calendar` USER_TZ.
`npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run lint` pass.
dependsOn: Create the pure goal-presentation registry module with fitness and project entries and hoisted formatters

title: Route legend.ts resolveLegend kind selection through presentation.legendDefault
value: so that the legend default and every other kind-aware surface share one source of kind→config truth instead of legend.ts carrying its own `kind === 'project'` branch
acceptanceCriteria: In src/lib/legend.ts, `resolveLegend` selects its default via `presentationForGoal(goal).legendDefault` ('fitness' → DEFAULT_LEGEND, 'project' → PROJECT_DEFAULT_LEGEND) rather than the inline `kind === 'project'` check (current lines ~98).
The stored-legend path is unchanged: a goal whose `legend` parses against LegendSchema still returns its own legend; a present-but-invalid legend still falls back to DEFAULT_LEGEND.
Fitness un-regressed: a fitness/null-kind goal with null legend still resolves to DEFAULT_LEGEND (byte-identical entries/order); the LegendKind enum and CalendarMonth.tsx coupling are untouched.
Project surfaces no longer mislabel: a project goal with null legend still resolves to PROJECT_DEFAULT_LEGEND via the registry.
goal-presentation.ts purity is preserved (legend.ts imports from it, not vice-versa; no Prisma/@lib/calendar pulled into the registry).
`npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run lint` pass.
dependsOn: Create the pure goal-presentation registry module with fitness and project entries and hoisted formatters

title: Gate the progress-page weight chart on weight-target presence and render an MRR trend for project goals
value: so that the progress page shows a weight chart only when a goal actually tracks weight, and shows a project-appropriate MRR-over-time trend for project goals instead of always rendering the fitness weight card
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/progress/page.tsx, the Weight card (lines ~177–202) renders only when the focus goal has a weight-backed target — concretely when its `targets[]` contains a `weightLb` metric (equivalently a fitness presentation slot), NOT a bare `kind === 'fitness'` check.
A fitness goal that has no `weightLb` target does NOT render an empty weight chart (the §8.5 mis-gate guardrail).

For a project focus goal, an MRR-over-time trend renders in place of the weight card: reuse MilestoneBurnDown (already self-gating at line ~173) and add an MRR sparkline sourced from a `logEntry` query on metric 'mrr' for the focus goal.
Chewgether (0 MRR LogEntry rows) renders the MRR trend in its honest empty state (no rows → 'no MRR logged yet'-style placeholder, not a broken/empty chart); milestone burn-down still shows 0/7.
Any date bucketing / axis math for the MRR trend goes through `@/lib/calendar` (USER_TZ), never raw Date/getDate/setHours.
Fitness un-regressed: for the fitness focus goal (with a weightLb target) the Weight card, WeightChart, current/start/Δ stats, and MilestoneBurnDown gating are pixel-identical to current production.
progress/page.tsx stays a server component; the MRR sparkline is a server-rendered (or already-client) chart with no Date instances crossing the server→client boundary.
`npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run lint` pass.
dependsOn: Create the pure goal-presentation registry module with fitness and project entries and hoisted formatters

title: Add a fitness counterpart coaching doc (docs/coaching/fitness-goal-prompts.md)
value: so that the coach's mental model is symmetric across verticals — documenting the fitness-shaped surfaces (ring=READINESS, stats=WORKOUTS/VOLUME/PRs/ELEVATION, rest-day copy, feasibility on Today/goal) the same way project-goal-prompts.md documents the project ones
acceptanceCriteria: New file docs/coaching/fitness-goal-prompts.md exists, mirroring the structure of the existing docs/coaching/project-goal-prompts.md.

It documents the fitness Today/progress/recap surfaces in fitness framing: ring label READINESS, recap stats WORKOUTS/VOLUME/PRs/ELEVATION, the rest-day recovery tip, the weight chart on progress, and that feasibility is surfaced on Today and the goal page.
It states (matching the registry) that fitness is the `__default__` clone, i.e. null/unknown-kind goals render fitness-shaped — without implying fitness is the universal 'correct' default.
Documentation only: no production code is touched; markdown is internally consistent with the surfaces this sprint ships.
Fitness un-regressed and project unaffected (doc-only change; no runtime impact).

title: Sprint 7 QA — fitness un-regressed + project surfaces correct at 390px (Today, progress, legend)

Project Today: no Mt. Elbert rest-day tip appears. Project /progress: no empty weight chart; MRR trend + milestone burn-down (0/7) render with honest empty states. Project legend resolves to PROJECT_DEFAULT_LEGEND.
Weight chart confirmed to gate on weight-target presence, not bare kind: a fitness goal without a weightLb target shows no empty weight card.
`npx tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run lint` clean, `npm run build` succeeds (SSR for / and /progress).

title: Build the goal-generic FeasibilityReadout server component
value: so that any goal's feasibility can be rendered honestly in the dashboard from a single GoalFeasibility, without an MCP round-trip and without re-hardcoding a vertical
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/components/FeasibilityReadout.tsx is a server component (no "use client" directive, no React hooks/state).

Component accepts an already-serialized GoalFeasibility prop; it never accepts or constructs a Date instance (GoalFeasibility.computedAt is already a string, weeksRemaining a number) — no Date crosses the server/client boundary.

Renders exactly four honest states selected purely from the GoalFeasibility value: (1) unratedReason==="someday" -> "No deadline set — feasibility unrated."; (2) unratedReason==="no-targets" -> "Add targets to rate feasibility."; (3) unratedReason==="no-data" (see two sub-states below); (4) tier set -> full readout with per-target requiredRate vs observedRate, weeksRemaining, verdict tier, and rateBasis.

The no-data state renders TWO distinct sub-states keyed on requiredRate===null: 0-log sub-state (requiredRate===null AND observedRate===null) -> copy can only say e.g. "Not enough logged data to rate yet — log the metric a few times" / "Add at least one entry to compute the pace you need" (it must NOT promise a per-week target, because requiredRate is null at zero logs per plan-critique C-1); 1–2-log sub-state (requiredRate populated, observedRate null) -> shows requiredRate (e.g. "needs ~$66/wk to hit $1,000 by 9/30") with observedRate rendered as "— not yet estimable".
Goal-generic: zero hardcoded references to fitness, Elbert, Chewgether, MRR, or any vertical-specific label — all labels derive from the GoalFeasibility perTarget entries.
Any date label (e.g. target date) is formatted via @/lib/calendar USER_TZ helpers, never a raw new Date()/toLocaleString without timeZone.
NO change to src/lib/rarity-core.ts: git diff --stat shows src/lib/rarity-core.ts is untouched by this story.

Chewgether criterion: rendered against the live Chewgether GoalFeasibility (0 logs) the component shows 'not enough logged data to rate' honestly today; rendered against a >=3-MRR-log fixture GoalFeasibility it shows a real verdict (tier + rateBasis "observed").
npx tsc --noEmit and npm run lint pass.

No Date instance is passed into FeasibilityReadout or any client child; feasibility is passed already-serialized.

Chewgether criterion: with a project focus goal, Today shows the honest no-data readout ('not enough logged data to rate') today; with the fitness focus goal, Today shows that goal's feasibility readout. Fitness Today is otherwise visually unchanged.
NO change to src/lib/rarity-core.ts (git diff --stat confirms).
npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, and npm run build pass.

Works for both a fitness goal (real readout) and the Chewgether project goal (honest no-data sub-state today).
No Date instance is passed to FeasibilityReadout; feasibility is passed already-serialized; any date label uses @/lib/calendar USER_TZ.

acceptanceCriteria: The get_goal tool *description* in src/lib/mcp/tools.ts gains one sentence advertising feasibility.computed — per-target requiredRate/observedRate/verdict, weeksRemaining, and unratedReason (someday|no-targets|no-data).
Only the description string changes for get_goal: its returned shape, handler logic, and computeGoalFeasibility are untouched.

docs/coaching/project-goal-prompts.md is updated to mention the FeasibilityReadout surface (Today + goal page) and that logging MRR >=3 times unlocks the observed-rate estimate (no-data is honest until then).
MCP curl tools/list shows the updated get_goal description and the same total tool count/names as before.

title: Verify the feasibility surface end-to-end across both verticals with no engine change

acceptanceCriteria: npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, and npm run build all pass on the integrated sprint.
MCP curl smoke (dev server running): get_goal on Chewgether returns feasibility.computed with unratedReason "no-data" (unchanged honest behavior) and the updated tool description.

Both no-data sub-states are observed: 0-log (requiredRate null) and a 1–2-log fixture (requiredRate populated, observedRate '— not yet estimable').
Final guardrail: git diff against the sprint base shows NO change to src/lib/rarity-core.ts anywhere in Sprint 8.

title: Unit-test progressFor + computeReadiness: gating ceiling 80, untested=0, coverage, decrease, already-met, build-from-zero
value: so that the readiness score is provably honest — a partially-logged or untested goal can never display a false 100%, gating targets verifiably cap the headline at 80, and every metric direction is scored correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/lib/readiness.test.ts; `npx vitest run` is green
progressFor: build-from-zero increase metrics (log:*, hike:*, workout:count) return current/target (covers the no-start accumulation path); target===0 returns null
progressFor: already-met case — increase current>=target returns 1, decrease current<=target returns 1 (even when start is below target)
progressFor: decrease comparative metric returns clamped (start-current)/(start-target); start===target returns 0; current===null returns null (untested)
computeReadiness: an open gating target forces ceiling=80 and score=Math.min(rawScore,80) — covers the gating-cap → ceiling 80 case; all gates cleared yields ceiling=100
computeReadiness: an untested target counts as 0 progress at full weight in the denominator (covers untested=0 — asserts NO false-100% when one of two equally-weighted targets is unlogged)
computeReadiness: coverage returns {tested,total} where tested counts only breakdown rows with progress!==null; missing[] and openGateCount are correct
computeReadiness: totalWeight===0 returns score 0 / rawScore 0 (no NaN)
resolveMetricValue/resolveMetricStart from @/lib/goal-targets are mocked (vi.mock) so cases are deterministic without a live DB; goalId/asOf are passed but never hit Prisma

title: Unit-test computeTargetFeasibility: log: observed path (>=3pts), <3pts unknown, met-check, ratioCap stall, decrease-sign
value: so that the feasibility verdict is provably honest for any numeric goal — a thin data set reads 'unknown / does not count' instead of a confident tier, a stalled metric reads as a fantasy rather than easy, and decrease metrics are not silently inverted.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/lib/rarity-core.test.ts; `npx vitest run` is green
Tests the EXISTING engine (computeTargetFeasibility in rarity-core.ts) — pure function, no source change and no mocking required

log: observed path: observedPoints>=3 with positive observedWeeklyRate (null norm family) yields rateBasis 'observed', plausibleRate===observedWeeklyRate, a real numeric ratio and a non-unknown verdict, countsTowardTier:true

Honest <3-points path: observedPoints of 0/1/2 with a null-norm family returns verdict 'unknown', countsTowardTier:false, ratio null, rateBasis 'none' (asserts minObservedPoints stays 3 — no false confidence from a 2-point slope)
Met-check: gap<=0 (current already at/past target, both directions) returns verdict 'met', ratio 0, requiredRate 0, countsTowardTier:true
ratioCap stall path: observedPoints>=3 with observedWeeklyRate<=0 and null norm returns ratio===ratioCap (99), verdict 'legendary', countsTowardTier:true (stalled = effectively impossible)
Decrease-sign normalization: a decrease metric passed a pre-normalized positive observedWeeklyRate is treated as improving (real ratio, not regression) — documents the caller's negate convention
Never-measured guard: current===null with no explicit target.start returns verdict 'unknown', countsTowardTier:false, requiredRate null

title: Unit-test the goal-presentation registry: per-kind config + __default__ fallback
value: so that the kind→presentation seam is pinned — fitness keeps its 4 recapField slots and READINESS ring, project resolves exactly 2 data-backed slots (MRR/MILESTONES) and a PROGRESS ring, and an unknown/null kind falls back to the labeled __default__ clone rather than mislabeling.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/lib/goal-presentation.test.ts; `npx vitest run` is green

presentationForGoal({kind:'fitness'}) returns ringLabel 'READINESS', headerStyle 'program-week', 4 statSlots (WORKOUTS/VOLUME/NEW PRs/ELEVATION) all source.from 'recapField', restCopy non-null, legendDefault 'fitness'

presentationForGoal({kind:'project'}) returns ringLabel 'PROGRESS' (NOT 'TRACTION'), headerStyle 'weeks-to-target', exactly 2 statSlots — MRR (logLatest 'mrr', currency) and MILESTONES (scheduledItem milestone doneOverTotal, ratioOfTotal), restCopy null, legendDefault 'project'

Unknown kind, null kind, and undefined goal all return the DEFAULT_PRESENTATION whose kind === '__default__' and whose slots match fitness (default-by-omission is the explicit labeled safety net, not 'fitness is universal')
Asserts project does NOT declare Subs/Conversion slots (the anti-vertical guardrail — only data-backed slots exist)
dependsOn: Create the pure goal-presentation registry module with fitness and project entries and hoisted formatters

title: Unit-test resolveStatSlot + recap statSlots derivation per kind (fitness byte-identical, project —/0/7)
value: so that the recap aggregator's per-kind stat derivation is provably correct — fitness slots resolve to byte-identical strings (no pixel regression on the live card) and a project goal honestly resolves MRR '—' (no logs) and MILESTONES '0/7' from ScheduledItem, never a fake $0 or a misleading dash for real milestones.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/lib/recap.test.ts; `npx vitest run` is green

resolveStatSlot (pure, fed a fabricated ctx — no DB): fitness recapField slots produce exactly the current strings via the hoisted formatters — String(n) for int, fmtVolume '2,370 lb' / '—', fmtElevation '5,200 ft' / '—' — with the same isNull flags (volumeLb===null, hikeElevationFt===null)
resolveStatSlot: project logLatest('mrr') with no logged value resolves to '—' isNull:true (honest dash, not '$0'); a present value formats as currency '$1,000'

resolveStatSlot: project scheduledItem(milestone, doneOverTotal) with {done:0,total:7} resolves to '0/7' isNull:false (sourced from the ScheduledItem aggregate, NOT log:milestones_done — the two-truths invariant)

Recap aggregator derivation: computeWeeklyRecap (Prisma mocked) emits statSlots whose length and values match the goal's presentation per kind — 4 fitness slots byte-identical to the legacy workoutsCompleted/volumeLb/prCount/hikeElevationFt fields (snapshot), 2 project slots resolving '—' and '0/7'
Legacy recap fields (workoutsCompleted/volumeLb/prCount/hikeElevationFt) remain present on the bundle alongside statSlots
dependsOn: Create the pure goal-presentation registry module with fitness and project entries and hoisted formatters
Add statSlots, resolveStatSlot, gated project fetch, and weeks-to-target header fields to computeWeeklyRecap

title: SPIKE: Proactive coach mechanism (scheduled routine vs. cron→MCP→nudge)
value: so that the coach can initiate (gate nudges, staleness alerts, auto Sunday recap) instead of only reacting — thread 3.3
acceptanceCriteria: A written spike doc compares Claude Code scheduled cloud routine (/schedule) vs. a cron job hitting MCP read tools (get_session_brief/compute_readiness/list_open_items) + a nudge/digest surface
Recommends a mechanism + a thin slice to build, with cost ($0 constraint) + USER_TZ + no-LLM-in-app implications
Decomposed into buildable stories as a follow-up — NOT built in the spike

title: EPIC: Close the content flywheel — auto Sunday recap → Instagram
value: so that the Weekly Recap Card becomes an automatic build-in-public habit that grows the audience at $0 — thread 3.4
acceptanceCriteria: Epic stub: auto-generate the Sunday card from the week's data (likely via the 3.3 scheduling mechanism)
Depends on the goal-kind-aware recap card (Sprint 6) so project + fitness cards both auto-post correctly
To be decomposed after Sprints 6–9 land

title: EPIC: Goal onboarding / the 'goal interview' for any domain
value: so that someone who isn't Gabe can turn a fuzzy goal into measurable, weighted, gated, feasibility-rated targets without hand-authoring targets[] JSON — thread 3.5; the usable-by-others unlock
acceptanceCriteria: Epic stub: the coach conducts a guided interview; goaldmine persists the result as a Goal + targets[] + gates
Must be goal-generic (fitness + project + future kinds) and honesty-first (gated, feasibility-rated)
Precondition for multi-user; to be decomposed after the platform identity (Sprints 6–9) lands

Elbert rest-day tip, and /progress shows the MRR trend + milestone burn-down instead of a weight chart — not just the feasibility readout documented in Sprint 8

Elbert rest-day tip; /progress shows the MRR-over-time trend + MilestoneBurnDown instead of a weight chart

title: Sprint 9 QA — full Vitest suite green + build, no production code modified by the test sprint
value: so that the honesty-math test sprint lands with the whole suite green on main, the build unbroken, and zero production-source drift — tests only
acceptanceCriteria: npx vitest run is green across the full committed suite (food-units + readiness + rarity-core + goal-presentation + recap specs)
npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, and npm run build all pass
git diff against the sprint base shows ONLY *.test.ts files added/changed — no production source modified by Sprint 9
Any failing assertion is filed back to the owning honesty-math story; the engine is not changed to make a test pass (per blueprint §3.1)
